title: Your Brain Can't Do Compound Interest — $400K Gone
hook: Scientists asked people to estimate compound interest and the average guess was off by 65% — that one cognitive glitch is why most people will work until they die.
angle: The 'exponential growth bias' is a documented cognitive flaw that makes humans systematically underestimate compound growth — it's the root cause of why 60% of 35-year-olds have under $25,000 saved.
audience: Anyone between 22-45 who keeps telling themselves they'll start saving more 'soon'
key_numbers: 65% — how far off the average person is when estimating compound returns
$1,744 — what $100 becomes at 10% for 30 years (most people guess $600)
$74,000 vs $37,000 — the gap between starting at 25 vs 35 with just $5,000
